stories.title: Miami GP Recap — Antonelli Wins Third Straight, Leclerc's Final-Lap Disaster

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kimi Antonelli: three pole positions, three wins.

The 19-year-old is making F1 history in 2026. Norris P2, Piastri P3.

Leclerc spun on the final lap, got a 20s penalty, finished P8. "All on me." #F1 #MiamiGP

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Championship after Miami:
Antonelli: 100 pts
Russell: 80 pts
Leclerc: 59 pts
Norris: 51 pts
Hamilton: 51 pts
Verstappen: 26 pts

The gap at the top is very real. #F1

facebook.long_form:
Kimi Antonelli just keeps winning — and the drama around him keeps getting wilder.

At the 2026 Miami Grand Prix on May 3, the 19-year-old Mercedes driver converted his third consecutive pole position into his third consecutive race victory, crossing the line 3.264 seconds ahead of Lando Norris in a McLaren that had genuinely closed the gap. Oscar Piastri completed the podium in third for a McLaren 2-3. George Russell was fourth for Mercedes, Max Verstappen fifth for Red Bull, and Lewis Hamilton sixth for Ferrari.

But the story everyone was talking about was Charles Leclerc. Running inside the top six, fighting for a strong Ferrari points haul, Leclerc spun at Turn 3 on the final lap — damaging his car and forcing him to cut chicanes to limp to the finish. The stewards added a 20-second time penalty for gaining a lasting advantage by leaving the track, dropping him from P6 to P8. Leclerc was characteristically honest: "It's all on me."

The championship picture after Miami: Antonelli 100, Russell 80, Leclerc 59, Norris 51, Hamilton 51, Verstappen 26. Mercedes leads constructors at 180 points.
facebook.image_caption: Three poles. Three wins. Antonelli is making history at 19. Leclerc's nightmare final lap gave him extra breathing room. Miami GP recap: Antonelli P1, Norris P2, Piastri P3. #MiamiGP #F1

article.headline: Antonelli Wins Third Straight Miami GP as Leclerc's Final-Lap Disaster Drops Ferrari to P8

stories.title: Verstappen at the Crossroads — Red Bull Crisis and the Exit Clause Ticking Down

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen: 7th in the championship, 74 points behind Antonelli.

His contract lets him leave Red Bull if he's outside the top 3 at the summer break.

That clause is almost certainly going to trigger. #F1 #Verstappen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Verstappen threatened to retire after Japan.

He's now 7th in the standings with a car he says is no fun to drive.

The summer break exit window opens soon. What comes next? #F1 #Verstappen

facebook.long_form:
Max Verstappen's situation at Red Bull is becoming increasingly dire — and a contractual escape route is almost certain to open up this summer.

The four-time world champion sits 7th in the 2026 drivers' championship with 26 points — 74 behind Antonelli. His contract with Red Bull contains a performance clause: if Verstappen is not inside the top three in the standings at the summer break (following the Hungarian Grand Prix on July 24-26), he has the right to terminate the contract early. He then has until October to formally notify the team.

With Mercedes, McLaren, Ferrari, and even Alpine all fielding cars that outperform the Red Bull regularly, the prospect of Verstappen ascending to the top three before Hungary appears virtually impossible unless the team makes massive gains.

Verstappen has been vocal about his frustrations with the 2026 regulations. The new power unit rules have produced cars he describes as difficult and un-fun to drive. Following the Japanese Grand Prix earlier in the season, he reportedly threatened retirement. His future is genuinely uncertain, and the exit clause countdown has begun.
facebook.image_caption: 7th in the championship. 74 points back. And a contract clause that could let him walk away by summer. Max Verstappen's 2026 season is a crisis. #Verstappen #F1

article.headline: Verstappen's Exit Clause is About to Be Triggered — What Happens Next at Red Bull?

stories.title: Canada GP Preview — Sprint Weekend in Montreal, May 22-24

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
NEXT RACE: Formula 1 Grand Prix du Canada 🇨🇦

May 22-24 at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve, Montreal.

Sprint weekend. Antonelli on a three-race win streak. McLaren improved. #CanadaGP #F1

facebook.long_form:
Formula 1 returns in just 12 days for one of the calendar's most beloved events — and this year it comes with extra stakes attached.

The 2026 Formula 1 Grand Prix du Canada takes place May 22-24 at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve on Île Notre-Dame in Montreal. This is a sprint format weekend, meaning extra points and extra drama: Sprint Qualifying on Friday, the Sprint Race at noon EDT on Saturday, followed by GP Qualifying at 4 PM EDT Saturday afternoon, and the Grand Prix on Sunday at 2 PM EDT.

Kimi Antonelli will arrive on a three-race winning streak leading the championship by 20 points over team-mate George Russell. McLaren, fresh from their best weekend of 2026 in Miami, are expected to bring more upgrades and could mount a genuine challenge. The Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve's distinctive mix of tight chicanes, a long hairpin, and the famous Wall of Champions punishes car imbalance and rewards precision — setting up a potential reshuffling of the competitive order.

Set your alarm for May 22, Montreal, and what should be another unforgettable F1 weekend.
facebook.image_caption: Next stop: Montreal. The 2026 #CanadaGP is a sprint weekend — May 22-24 at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve. Antonelli arrives on a 3-race win streak. Can anyone stop him? #F1

article.headline: Canada GP Preview: Sprint Weekend in Montreal Could Reshape the 2026 Championship Fight

stories.title: McLaren's Seven-Part Upgrade Unlocks New Form — Can They Challenge in Canada?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
McLaren brought seven upgrades to Miami — front wing, nose, sidepods, engine cover, floor, rear wing.

Norris P2. Piastri P3.

Stella says "execution and optimisation" was the only thing that kept them from winning. #McLaren #F1

facebook.long_form:
McLaren arrived in Miami with the most comprehensive upgrade package of the 2026 season — and the results spoke for themselves.

The MCL40 received seven changes heading into the Miami weekend, covering the front wing, nose cone, sidepods, engine cover, floor, and rear wing. The goal, according to team principal Andrea Stella, was not just raw pace — it was also widening the car's operating window and making it more predictable for Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri.

It worked. Norris won the Sprint on Saturday (his first win of 2026), and both drivers delivered in the race: Norris P2, Piastri P3. Their double podium was the clearest sign yet that the gap to Mercedes is closing. Stella acknowledged that "execution and optimisation" — not pace — was what kept McLaren from winning outright.

Piastri called the upgrade results "a pretty big surprise" given their pre-weekend expectations, adding optimism about Canada. Norris cautioned the team needs more data points, but expressed confidence that the improvements are real.
facebook.image_caption: Seven upgrades. One sprint win. One podium double. McLaren is finding their 2026 form at exactly the right time. Norris P2, Piastri P3 in Miami. #McLaren #F1

article.headline: McLaren's Seven-Part Miami Upgrade Has Reawakened a Championship Contender

stories.title: Antonelli's Historic Start — Three Poles, Three Wins, 100 Points

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
No F1 driver in history had ever converted their first three pole positions of a season into race wins.

Kimi Antonelli just did it at 19 years old.

The 2026 championship is his to lose. #Antonelli #F1

facebook.long_form:
The numbers coming out of the 2026 F1 season are almost incomprehensible when you remember that Kimi Antonelli is 19 years old.

Three pole positions in 2026. Three race wins. 100 championship points. Leading the standings by 20 points over team-mate George Russell. And — according to Formula 1 itself — the first driver in the sport's history to convert his first three pole positions of a season into victories.

Antonelli's rise has been remarkable not just for the results but for the manner of them. In Miami, he recovered from a difficult first corner to reassert control of the race through strategy, then held off a charging Norris in a tense finale. His racecraft under pressure belies his age. Mercedes leads the constructors' championship with 180 points — 68 ahead of Ferrari.

With the Canada Grand Prix coming on May 22-24, Antonelli has an opportunity to extend his advantage further on a circuit where he's never raced. The 2026 championship is his to lose.
facebook.image_caption: 19 years old. 3 poles. 3 wins. 100 points. Leading the championship. Kimi Antonelli is making F1 history in 2026. #Antonelli #F1

article.headline: Kimi Antonelli: Three Poles, Three Wins, 100 Points — The Most Dominant F1 Start Since the Records Began
